commands.review_summary:
This PR adds Resend-backed moderation emails, routes banned sign-in copy to the appeal site, and changes ClawScan-malicious skill/plugin handling to quarantine candidate versions before thresholded account bans.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature and policy PR. The supplied proof and source inspection show the new email rendering and banned-account sign-in copy paths rather than a failing current-main bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs human maintainer/security acceptance of the malware threshold policy, Resend supply-chain alert, and production email env rollout; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat: improve account appeal notifications
target.body: ## Summary This PR turns scanner-triggered malware handling into a coherent two-level flow: - artifact-level scanner rejections quarantine the specific skill/plugin version and email the publisher remediation guidance - existing clean latest versions stay live; malicious first versions/releases do not publish a public latest - account-level bans and Hermit appeals stay reserved for account-level enforcement after repeated malicious rejections - banned-account sign-in errors now point users at the appeal flow instead of looking like generic login trouble - Resend-backed account emails cover bans, restores, and malicious-artifact warnings ## User-Facing Proof Proof comment with uploaded artifacts: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/pull/2520#issuecomment-4636295683 ### Rendered Emails Rendered from the real `convex/lib/emails.ts` builders. Left is the account-level ban email with the Hermit appeal link; right is the artifact-level scanner rejection email with local `clawhub scan` guidance and the repeated-rejection warning. These are email HTML outputs, not ClawHub app routes. ### Banned Sign-In Copy Captured from a full-stack local ClawHub preview via `bun run test:pw:local-auth` on `/dashboard?error_description=Account%20banned` with local Convex. The e2e proof asserts the inline dashboard alert and its `https://appeals.openclaw.ai/` appeal link. ## Flow Diagrams ### Artifact-Level Scanner Rejection ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Publisher uploads skill/plugin version] --> B[ClawHub security scan] B -->|clean or non-blocking| C[Promote version normally] B -->|malicious| D[Quarantine candidate version/release] D --> E{Existing clean latest?} E -->|yes| F[Keep previous clean latest public] E -->|no| G[No public latest is promoted] D --> H[Email publisher: artifact blocked, account still active] H --> I[Publisher runs clawhub scan on fixed local copy] I --> J[Publisher uploads fixed version] D --> K{Escalation threshold crossed?} K -->|no| L[No account appeal path] K -->|yes| M[Account-level autoban] ``` ### Account-Level Ban + Hermit Appeal ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Account is banned: manual or repeated malicious rejections] --> B[ClawHub sends account ban email] A --> C[Banned sign-in attempt] B --> D[Appeal link: appeals.openclaw.ai] C --> D D --> E[Hermit appeal tool handles account-level review] E -->|accepted| F[ClawHub appeal service unbans/restores eligible listings] E -->|rejected| G[Account remains banned] ``` ## Behavioral Notes - Artifact warning emails intentionally do not include an appeal link. - The artifact email tells users their account can still sign in, the rejected version was not made public, they can upload a fixed version, and: “Repeated malicious rejections may lead to account disablement.” - Account ban emails include the affected skill/plugin when known, a high-level reason, the Hermit appeal link, and scan-result guidance when the ban came from scanner escalation. - Rate-limit/manual bans do not get scanner remediation copy. ## Tests / Verification - `bunx vitest run convex/lib/emails.test.ts convex/users.test.ts convex/skills.rateLimit.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts` — 275 tests passed - `bun run ci:static` — passed - `bun run ci:unit` — 2894 tests passed, 1 skipped - `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed - `PLAYWRIGHT_LOCAL_AUTH_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3310 PLAYWRIGHT_LOCAL_AUTH_CONVEX_SITE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3311 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=4176 bun run test:pw:local-auth -- --project=chromium e2e/pr-2520-banned-auth-proof.pw.test.ts` — passed locally with a temporary proof test; captured the live dashboard screenshot, then removed the temp test from the branch
